summary: P0 (SuperAdmin & Limits), P2 (SuperAdmin Dashboard), and Subscriptions features fully tested. Backend: 16/16 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All pages verified. Fixed MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug in subscribe endpoint.

issues: React BrowserRouter warning about setState during render in Login component - minor warning

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/dashboard/superadmin-stats returns 200 for superadmin
Backend: GET /api/dashboard/superadmin-stats returns correct users structure (total, by_role, recent)
Backend: GET /api/dashboard/superadmin-stats returns correct resources structure (suppliers, products, catalogs, woocommerce_stores)
Backend: GET /api/dashboard/superadmin-stats returns correct sync structure (this_week, errors_this_week)
Backend: GET /api/dashboard/superadmin-stats returns 403 for non-superadmin
Backend: GET /api/users returns list of users (without passwords)
Backend: GET /api/users/{user_id} returns user with usage details
Backend: PUT /api/users/{user_id}/limits updates user limits (superadmin only)
Backend: PUT /api/users/{user_id}/role changes user role
Backend: GET /api/subscriptions/plans returns 4 plans (Free, Starter, Professional, Enterprise)
Backend: Plans have correct structure (id, name, description, limits, prices, features)
Backend: Free plan has zero prices
Backend: Professional plan has correct limits (50 suppliers, 20 catalogs, 10 stores)
Backend: Enterprise plan has unlimited limits (999999)
Backend: GET /api/subscriptions/my returns current subscription
Backend: POST /api/subscriptions/subscribe/{plan_id} creates subscription
Frontend: Sidebar shows 'Usuarios', 'Dashboard Admin', 'Suscripciones' menu items for superadmin
Frontend: User Management page loads with title 'Gestión de Usuarios'
Frontend: Users table shows 6 users with role selectors and limit buttons
Frontend: Edit Limits dialog opens with all three input fields
Frontend: SuperAdmin Dashboard shows global stats (6 Users, 11,577 Products, 5 Syncs, 2 Errors)
Frontend: SuperAdmin Dashboard shows charts (Users by Role, Resources)
Frontend: SuperAdmin Dashboard shows WooCommerce stats, Top users, Recent users
Frontend: Subscriptions page shows 4 plan cards with prices
Frontend: Billing toggle switches between Mensual (€19.99/mes) and Anual (€199.99/año)
Frontend: Professional plan has 'Más Popular' badge
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug in /api/subscriptions/subscribe endpoint - _id was being returned after insert_one
All subscription plans auto-create with sensible defaults when none exist
seed_data_creation: 4 subscription plans auto-created on first /api/subscriptions/plans call
context_for_next_testing_agent: SuperAdmin features (P0, P2) and Subscriptions fully working. User test@test.com has role=superadmin with unlimited limits (999999). Admin user admin@test.com exists with Starter subscription. Subscription endpoints use MongoDB ObjectId exclusion pattern: find_one({...}, {"_id": 0}) and subscription_doc.pop("_id", None) after insert_one.
rca_of_the_issue: MongoDB ObjectId Serialization Bug - POST /api/subscriptions/subscribe was returning MongoDB ObjectId in response. Root cause: insert_one() modifies the original dict in-place adding _id field. Fix: Added subscription_doc.pop('_id', None) before returning and added {"_id": 0} projection to find_one for plan.
